members.name: GenerateTriangleMesh
members.summary: Returns a [TriangleMesh] with the label's vertices following its current configuration (such as its [member pixel_size]).

members.name: GetDrawFlag
members.summary: Returns the value of the specified flag.

members.name: SetDrawFlag
members.signature: public void SetDrawFlag(int flag, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the specified [param flag] will be enabled.

members.name: AlphaAntialiasingEdge
members.summary: Threshold at which antialiasing will be applied on the alpha channel.

members.name: AlphaAntialiasingMode
members.summary: The type of alpha antialiasing to apply.

members.name: AlphaCut
members.summary: The alpha cutting mode to use for the sprite.

members.name: AlphaHashScale
members.summary: The hashing scale for Alpha Hash. Recommended values between [code]0[/code] and [code]2[/code].

members.name: AlphaScissorThreshold
members.summary: Threshold at which the alpha scissor will discard values.

members.name: AutowrapMode
members.summary: If set to something other than [constant TextServer.AUTOWRAP_OFF], the text gets wrapped inside the node's bounding rectangle. If you resize the node, it will change its height automatically to show all the text.

members.name: AutowrapTrimFlags
members.summary: Autowrap space trimming flags. See [constant TextServer.BREAK_TRIM_START_EDGE_SPACES] and [constant TextServer.BREAK_TRIM_END_EDGE_SPACES] for more info.

members.name: Billboard
members.summary: The billboard mode to use for the label.

members.name: DoubleSided
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], text can be seen from the back as well, if [code]false[/code], it is invisible when looking at it from behind.

members.name: FixedSize
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the label is rendered at the same size regardless of distance. The label's size on screen is the same as if the camera was [code]1.0[/code] units away from the label's origin, regardless of the actual distance from the camera. The [Camera3D]'s field of view (or [member Camera3D.size] when in orthogonal/frustum mode) still affects the size the label is drawn at.

members.name: Font
members.summary: Font configuration used to display text.

members.name: FontSize
members.summary: Font size of the [Label3D]'s text. To make the font look more detailed when up close, increase [member font_size] while decreasing [member pixel_size] at the same time. Higher font sizes require more time to render new characters, which can cause stuttering during gameplay.

members.name: HorizontalAlignment
members.summary: Controls the text's horizontal alignment. Supports left, center, right, and fill (also known as justify).

members.name: JustificationFlags
members.summary: Line fill alignment rules.

members.name: Language
members.summary: Language code used for line-breaking and text shaping algorithms. If left empty, the current locale is used instead.

members.name: LineSpacing
members.summary: Additional vertical spacing between lines (in pixels), spacing is added to line descent. This value can be negative.

members.name: Modulate
members.summary: Text [Color] of the [Label3D].

members.name: NoDepthTest
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], depth testing is disabled and the object will be drawn in render order.

members.name: Offset
members.summary: The text drawing offset (in pixels).

members.name: OutlineModulate
members.summary: The tint of text outline.

members.name: OutlineRenderPriority
members.summary: Sets the render priority for the text outline. Higher priority objects will be sorted in front of lower priority objects. [b]Note:[/b] This only applies if [member alpha_cut] is set to [constant ALPHA_CUT_DISABLED] (default value). [b]Note:[/b] This only applies to sorting of transparent objects. This will not impact how transparent objects are sorted relative to opaque objects. This is because opaque objects are not sorted, while transparent objects are sorted from back to front (subject to priority).

members.name: OutlineSize
members.summary: Text outline size.

members.name: PixelSize
members.summary: The size of one pixel's width on the label to scale it in 3D. To make the font look more detailed when up close, increase [member font_size] while decreasing [member pixel_size] at the same time.

members.name: RenderPriority
members.summary: Sets the render priority for the text. Higher priority objects will be sorted in front of lower priority objects. [b]Note:[/b] This only applies if [member alpha_cut] is set to [constant ALPHA_CUT_DISABLED] (default value). [b]Note:[/b] This only applies to sorting of transparent objects. This will not impact how transparent objects are sorted relative to opaque objects. This is because opaque objects are not sorted, while transparent objects are sorted from back to front (subject to priority).

members.name: Shaded
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Light3D] in the [Environment] has effects on the label.

members.name: StructuredTextBidiOverride
members.summary: Set BiDi algorithm override for the structured text.

members.name: StructuredTextBidiOverrideOptions
members.summary: Set additional options for BiDi override.

members.name: Text
members.summary: The text to display on screen.

members.name: TextDirection
members.summary: Base text writing direction.

members.name: TextureFilter
members.summary: Filter flags for the texture.

members.name: Uppercase
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], all the text displays as UPPERCASE.

members.name: VerticalAlignment
members.summary: Controls the text's vertical alignment. Supports top, center, and bottom.

members.name: Width
members.summary: Text width (in pixels), used for autowrap and fill alignment.

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_DISABLED
values.summary: This mode performs standard alpha blending. It can display translucent areas, but transparency sorting issues may be visible when multiple transparent materials are overlapping. [member GeometryInstance3D.cast_shadow] has no effect when this transparency mode is used; the [Label3D] will never cast shadows.

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_DISCARD
values.summary: This mode only allows fully transparent or fully opaque pixels. Harsh edges will be visible unless some form of screen-space antialiasing is enabled (see [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/screen_space_aa]). This mode is also known as [i]alpha testing[/i] or [i]1-bit transparency[/i]. [b]Note:[/b] This mode might have issues with anti-aliased fonts and outlines, try adjusting [member alpha_scissor_threshold] or using MSDF font. [b]Note:[/b] When using text with overlapping glyphs (e.g., cursive scripts), this mode might have transparency sorting issues between the main text and the outline.

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_HASH
values.summary: This mode draws cuts off all values below a spatially-deterministic threshold, the rest will remain opaque.

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_OPAQUE_PREPASS
values.summary: This mode draws fully opaque pixels in the depth prepass. This is slower than [constant ALPHA_CUT_DISABLED] or [constant ALPHA_CUT_DISCARD], but it allows displaying translucent areas and smooth edges while using proper sorting. [b]Note:[/b] When using text with overlapping glyphs (e.g., cursive scripts), this mode might have transparency sorting issues between the main text and the outline.

values.name: FLAG_DISABLE_DEPTH_TEST
values.summary: Disables the depth test, so this object is drawn on top of all others. However, objects drawn after it in the draw order may cover it.

values.name: FLAG_DOUBLE_SIDED
values.summary: If set, text can be seen from the back as well. If not, the text is invisible when looking at it from behind.

values.name: FLAG_FIXED_SIZE
values.summary: Label is scaled by depth so that it always appears the same size on screen.

values.name: FLAG_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DrawFlags] enum.

values.name: FLAG_SHADED
values.summary: If set, lights in the environment affect the label.

constants.name: FLAG_SHADED
constants.summary: If set, lights in the environment affect the label.

constants.name: FLAG_DOUBLE_SIDED
constants.summary: If set, text can be seen from the back as well. If not, the text is invisible when looking at it from behind.

constants.name: FLAG_DISABLE_DEPTH_TEST
constants.summary: Disables the depth test, so this object is drawn on top of all others. However, objects drawn after it in the draw order may cover it.

constants.name: FLAG_FIXED_SIZE
constants.summary: Label is scaled by depth so that it always appears the same size on screen.

constants.name: FLAG_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DrawFlags] enum.
